taxonomy: Agathisanthemum globosum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Agathisanthemum, which contains several species of flowering plants native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Africa. The classification of Agathisanthemum globosum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Rubiaceae family.

intro: Agathisanthemum globosum, also known as globe bush, is a plant species native to tropical Africa that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. Traditionally, Agathisanthemum globosum has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
